description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking blue fuselage and dual wings, each adorned with roundel insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane features a prominent radial engine at the nose, with a metallic propeller and exposed engine details. The tail section is decorated with a tricolor rudder and a horizontal stabilizer, while the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield for the pilot. Tensioned wires connect the wings and provide structural support, and the overall design is streamlined for early aviation performance. The surface finish is smooth and glossy, with visible metallic and painted elements.
description: A classic blue biplane with dual wings, roundel insignias, an open cockpit, and a radial engine with a metallic propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radial engine, propeller, and symmetrical dual wings with roundel insignias.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open cockpit, wing struts, and tricolor tail rudder.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizer, and the alignment of the dual wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and the roundel insignia on the upper wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts and tensioned wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; tricolor rudder present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing; appears to be single-seat with a small windshield.
